name: Feed-Bait Maximalist
rationale: Renders the Feed-Wraith's domain in the actual visual grammar of platform virality — the TikTok / Reels / YouTube thumbnail: blown-out saturation, shocked reaction faces, red arrows and circles, giant caption bars, the 'YOU WON'T BELIEVE' scream. Deployed satirically and against itself: the loud, hollow bait crashes into the warm painted fellowship and visibly dies, so the style is the punchline. It captures click-bait engagement-spam as a graphic language without the leering, sexualized register that grammar usually trades in — the joke is the desperation for attention, not titillation.
preamble: Maximalist viral-thumbnail / engagement-bait graphic style, used satirically; hyper-saturated punch-up color grading, hard drop-shadows, and crunchy over-sharpened HDR; oversized bold caption bars in screaming sans-serif ('YOU WON'T BELIEVE', 'GONE WRONG'), giant red arrows, red circles, and glowing outlines pointing at everything; exaggerated open-mouthed shocked reaction faces, emoji and reaction stickers, fake play-buttons, view-count and notification overlays; cheap lens-flare, zoom-burst, and confetti-spam energy; mood: desperate dopamine-spike attention-farming, loud and hollow and already flickering out; references: MrBeast-era YouTube thumbnails, TikTok/Reels text-on-clip overlays, tabloid clickbait layouts; keep it garish and comedic, never sexualized — the satire targets the hunger for the click, and the bait is always shown crashing into and losing to the warm, hand-painted fellowship beside it.
